options.title: A. Publish current STAGED 0.1.2
options.summary: Fastest path, but it ships the technical validation build rather than the commercial packaging and payment-ready revision.
pros: No re-review wait for the current approved package
Technically proven upload and review path
cons: Misses the intended commercial packaging and monetization entry points
Would publish a version the user explicitly does not want to ship

options.title: B. Submit 0.2.0 commercial revision
options.summary: Best match for the current goal: keep 0.1.2 as proof the review path works, then resubmit the commercial candidate.
pros: Keeps the approved STAGED run as evidence the review path works
Allows payment entry, license activation, premium packaging, and updated listing assets
cons: Needs human visual review and dashboard sync before any upload approval

options.title: C. Hold STAGED and market-test first
options.summary: Useful if pricing or commercial positioning still needs more external proof before re-review.
pros: Lets the team test demand using landing and fake-door assets first
cons: Delays the commercial Chrome Web Store submission
Still does not ship the commercial bundle

why: Because the user wants payment entry, license activation, premium packaging, and store-ready assets before release, the right move is to keep 0.1.2 STAGED as proof and submit 0.2.0 as the commercial candidate.
next_step: Finish premium packaging, human visual review, and dashboard listing sync, then decide whether to request sandbox upload approval for 0.2.0.
